ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "current_population" INTEGER /* Current population of the country */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density (people per square kilometer) */,
  "urban_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in urban areas */,
  "world_population_share" REAL /* Country's share of the world population */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the country by population */,
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the continent */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Name of the capital city */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Type of government */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_continent_id FOREIGN KEY ("continent_id") REFERENCES continents ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "yearly_percentage_change" REAL /* Yearly percentage change in population */,
  "yearly_change" INTEGER /* Yearly change in population */,
  "migrants_net" INTEGER /* Net number of migrants */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Fertility rate */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Urban population */,
  "world_population" INTEGER /* World population */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Average life expectancy */,
  "birth_rate" REAL /* Birth rate per 1,000 population */,
  "death_rate" REAL /* Death rate per 1,000 population */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in poverty */,
  "literacy_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the population that is literate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "migrants" (
  "migrant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each migrant data entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population data entry */,
  "net_migrants" INTEGER /* Net number of migrants */,
  "in_migrants" INTEGER /* Number of incoming migrants */,
  "out_migrants" INTEGER /* Number of outgoing migrants */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("migrant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migrants_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "urban_data" (
  "urban_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each urban data entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population data entry */,
  "urban_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in urban areas */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Urban population */,
  "urban_growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the urban population */,
  "urban_area" REAL /* Total area of urban regions in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("urban_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_urban_data_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fertility_data" (
  "fertility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fertility data entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population data entry */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Fertility rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fertility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fertility_data_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "density_data" (
  "density_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each density data entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population data entry */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density (people per square kilometer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("density_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_density_data_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "world_population" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "world_population" INTEGER /* World population */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data entry accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data entry changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., insert, update, delete) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES population_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "continents" (
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each continent */,
  "continent_name" TEXT /* Name of the continent */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the continent in square kilometers */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the continent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "native_speakers" INTEGER /* Number of native speakers */,
  "official_status" TEXT /* Whether the language is official in the country (YES/NO) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_languages" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the language */,
  "percentage_speakers" REAL /* Percentage of the population that speaks the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id", "language_id")
)
